title: uva-ursi myrtifolia
intro: Uva-ursi myrtifolia, also known as bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the northern hemisphere. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of arbutin, a glycoside that has been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The health benefits of uva-ursi myrtifolia include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Uva-ursi myrtifolia can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including kidney stones and digestive problems.
